reasoning: The document demonstrates strong content fidelity with high programmatic shingling recall (0.983) and a high composite fidelity score (0.9005). While minor structural issues exist—including inconsistent heading hierarchy in the second half of the document and the omission of two specific URLs—these defects are cosmetic or localized and do not undermine the overall utility of the document according to the established scoring bands.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: There are two instances where specific URLs present in the source text are completely missing from the output, likely due to identification as separate elements that were then dropped. Specifically, 'www.ncstreams.net' is missing from Section 2, and 'www.nconemap.net' is missing from Section 7. These omissions represent a loss of functional information provided in the source.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is significantly corrupted in the latter half of the document. Under the 'Committee Reports' section (H2, element 54), the subsections are handled inconsistently. Earlier sub-committees (SMAC, SGUC, LGC) are rendered as paragraphs with bold/italic formatting. Later sub-committees (FIC, GIS TAC, M&O) are promoted to Level 2 headings (elements 74, 75, 76), making them siblings rather than children of 'Committee Reports'. The FIC will continue to discuss land use planning around military installations in North Carolina as directed by the GICC.

Mary Combs reported on the NAIP photography (leaf-on) options for 2008 and the fact that North Carolina will not be flown in 2007. The FIC will continue to discuss land use planning around military installations in North Carolina as directed by the GICC."

concerns.analysis: The numbered lists used for recommendations (e.g., (1) through (9) on pages 4-5) are converted to 'unordered' list types in the JSON (elements 40, 42). While the explicit numbers are preserved within the text, the semantic list type is incorrect. Additionally, Action items (Action 1-5) are handled inconsistently: some are converted into single-item unordered lists (elements 49, 51), while others are left as paragraphs (elements 65, 73, 82).
